replay.label: Quick start tour
replay.description: Replay the guided walkthrough of the Oratorio board.

welcome.title: Welcome to Oratorio
welcome.body: Oratorio is a board where you hand work to your AI agent — DotCraft — and watch it go. Before you start, make sure DotCraft is already set up for your project.

columns.title: The rhythm of the board
columns.body: Four columns follow the natural flow of work: To do → In progress → In review → Done. Every card moves left to right as your agent picks it up and finishes it.

connectProject.title: Connect your project
connectProject.body: Open Settings → GitHub or GitLab and point Oratorio at the same folder DotCraft is configured for. One connection, one save.

credentials.title: Pull in real work (optional)
credentials.body: Connect a source under Settings → GitHub or GitLab and Oratorio mirrors your issues, PRs, and MRs as cards. Skip this to just try the board with your own tasks.

newTask.title: Write your first task
newTask.body: Click New task to add a local one — a title, a short description, and the project you connected. The card lands in the To do column.

handOff.title: Hand it off to the agent
handOff.body: Drag a card from To do into In progress. Oratorio gives the agent a safe copy of your project, DotCraft picks up the task, and progress streams back into the card.

review.title: Review and decide
review.body: When a card reaches In review, open it to read the agent's summary, inline suggestions, and comments. Then choose: Approve, Ask for changes, or Reject.

next.title: You're ready
next.body: That's a full cycle — task created, agent worked, you decided. You can replay this tour anytime from Settings → General.
